title: Add optional git block to TC record schema

description.summary: Extend the tc_record schema with an optional `git` block capturing commit SHAs, branch info, remotes, and PR metadata. Foundation for all subsequent git integration work (TC-009 through TC-016).
description.motivation: The TC system was entirely VCS-agnostic, with /tc retro --from-git as the sole git touchpoint. To enable commit-level linking, auto-link hooks, PR enrichment, and git-aware dashboards, records must carry git metadata. Designed as an optional top-level object so the 7 existing TCs remain valid with zero migration.
description.detailed_design: Added top-level `git` property to tc_record.schema.json: { repo_root, initial_branch, commits[], remotes[], release_tags[] }. Each commit entry is append-only with {sha, short_sha, author, authored_date, subject, branch, parent_count, files_changed[], linked_at, link_source}. Each remote has optional nested pr object with {number, url, state, merged_sha, review_decision, last_synced}. Validator enforces SHA regex ^[0-9a-f]{7,40}$, deduplicates SHAs within a record, and validates provider enum (github|gitlab|bitbucket|other) and PR state enum (open|merged|closed|draft). Backward compatible: absent/null git block passes validation.

files_affected.description: Added optional top-level `git` object with commits, remotes, and PR metadata
Added _validate_git_block() function, SHA regex constants, provider/link_source/PR state enums, and wired into validate_tc_record()

revision_history.author: ai:claude-opus-4-6
revision_history.summary: TC created: add optional git block to tc_record schema as foundation for git integration (Phase 1 of 8-TC plan).

revision_history.author: ai:claude-opus-4-6
revision_history.summary: Schema and validator updates complete. All 7 existing TCs still validate. Seven positive/negative test cases on _validate_git_block pass (None, empty, full valid, bad SHA, duplicate SHA, bad provider, bad PR state).

field_changes.reason: Schema + validator code written, backward compat verified, unit cases pass

test_cases.title: Existing TCs remain valid after schema extension
procedure: Run `python validators/validate_tc.py docs/TC/records/TC-NNN-*/tc_record.json` for all 7 pre-existing TCs
Confirm each prints 'VALID' with exit code 0
test_cases.expected_result: All 7 existing TC records pass validation with no errors (backward compatibility preserved — git block is optional)
test_cases.actual_result: All 7 printed VALID: TC-001 through TC-007 inclusive

evidence.description: Validator output for all 7 existing TCs
evidence.content:
=== TC-001 === VALID
=== TC-002 === VALID
=== TC-003 === VALID
=== TC-004 === VALID
=== TC-005 === VALID
=== TC-006 === VALID
=== TC-007 === VALID

test_cases.title: _validate_git_block accepts None and empty dict
test_cases.expected_result: Both calls return empty error lists; no exceptions raised

test_cases.title: _validate_git_block accepts a fully populated valid block
procedure: Construct a git dict with repo_root, initial_branch, one commit, one remote with a PR, and release_tags
test_cases.expected_result: Fully populated valid block returns no errors

test_cases.title: _validate_git_block rejects malformed SHA
procedure: Build a commit entry with sha='notasha'
test_cases.expected_result: Validator emits error about SHA format (regex ^[0-9a-f]{7,40}$)

test_cases.title: _validate_git_block detects duplicate SHAs within a record
procedure: Build git.commits with two entries sharing sha='abc1234'
test_cases.expected_result: Validator rejects duplicate SHAs (preserves 1 TC ↔ 1 commit linkage invariant)

test_cases.title: _validate_git_block rejects unknown provider and PR state
procedure: Build remotes with provider='gitea' (not in enum)
Build pr with state='reopened' (not in enum)
test_cases.expected_result: Validator emits enum errors for both provider and PR state
test_cases.actual_result: Case 6 (bad provider): PASS; Case 7 (bad PR state): PASS

evidence.content:
Case 6 (bad provider): PASS
Case 7 (bad PR state): PASS

handoff.progress_summary: TC-008 complete: schema extended with optional git block, validator updated with _validate_git_block(), all 7 existing TCs still validate, 7 positive/negative test cases on the new validator pass. Foundation ready for TC-009 onward.
next_steps: TC-009: implement generators/tc_git_link.py (/tc link command) and tc_git_status.py (/tc git status)
TC-010: post-commit auto-link hook script and settings template
TC-013: session lifecycle hooks (SessionStart, Stop, PreCompact)
TC-014: online /tc pr link and /tc sync (gh/glab, opt-in)
TC-015: dashboard + per-TC HTML git rendering with CSS-only badges
key_context: User confirmed: default offline, online features opt-in
All generators must stay Python stdlib only
HTML must remain CSS-only (WCAG AA+, rem fonts, dark theme)
Cross-agent compat: no subagent-only features — Cursor/Codex/Gemini users must have equivalent path
Revision history is append-only; git.commits[] follows the same rule
SHA dedup enforced at validator level

decisions_made.decision: Git block is top-level optional object, not nested inside metadata
decisions_made.rationale: Top-level placement lets /tc git status and HTML generators find it without digging, and matches the precedent of files_affected[] and session_context being siblings of metadata.

decisions_made.decision: Enforce SHA uniqueness within git.commits[] at validator level
decisions_made.rationale: Prevents accidental double-linking from auto-hook + manual /tc link. Duplicate detection is cheap (set) and the error message is clearer than catching it downstream.

decisions_made.decision: PR block is nested under remotes[], not top-level git.pr
decisions_made.rationale: A TC could theoretically span forks or mirror remotes. Nesting PR under the remote keeps the data model correct if that ever happens, and degrades cleanly to a single origin remote in the common case.

notes: Part of the 9-TC git integration initiative. TC-008 is the schema foundation; TC-009 through TC-016 build commands, hooks, merge drivers, online PR sync, and dashboards on top of this block. Decision log: user confirmed default-offline with online as opt-in feature, so all downstream TCs must degrade gracefully if gh/glab unavailable and never assume network.
